objective: Create pilot infrastructure — hardware profile schema, pilot run orchestrator script, and pilot plan documentation — in preparation for the physical multi-device pilot (M9).

pilot_infrastructure: scripts/pilot/hardware-profile.schema.json — JSON schema defining required fields for device hardware profiles (deviceName, os, cpu, ram, vram, runtimeProvider, availableModels, advertisedCapabilities, networkAddress)
scripts/pilot/hardware-profile-template.json — template with placeholder comments for operators to fill in

scripts/pilot/pilot-runner.js — CLI script that executes track workflows with three relay policies (local-only, local-first, distributed), collects timing metrics and relay placement evidence, writes per-run JSON evidence files and summary CSV
docs/05-integrations/multi-device-pilot.md — pilot plan document covering prerequisites, setup, run procedures, evidence collection, and tear-down
package_scripts: pilot:local-only — runs pilot with local-only relay policy
pilot:local-first — runs pilot with local-first relay policy
pilot:distributed — runs pilot with distributed relay policy
tests: All existing tests pass: benchmark:test, benchmark:status-smoke, contract-test, test-relay-unit.cjs (52/52), test-relay-placement.cjs (17/17)
Pilot runner tested in local-only mode against running Local Brain with mock provider — successfully executes tracks and writes evidence

results: hardware-profile.schema.json validates against JSON Schema draft-07
hardware-profile-template.json contains all required fields with placeholder values
pilot-runner.js runs without error in local-only mode against a running Local Brain (mock provider)
Pilot runner collects per-run timing metrics (total_duration_ms), relay_placement summary, and writes evidence JSON files
Pilot runner writes summary CSV with columns: policy, workflow, run_number, total_duration_ms, ok, relay_nodes_used
docs/05-integrations/multi-device-pilot.md exists covering prerequisites, setup, run procedures, evidence collection, and tear-down
package.json has pilot:local-only, pilot:local-first, pilot:distributed scripts
No existing files outside the defined scope are modified
